description: Supine or half-kneeling: bring ribs down and pelvis into slight posterior tilt to re-establish the canister, then full exhalation to feel anterior core engage. Foundational for rib-cage control.
notes: Step 1 = ribs down + posterior tilt; step 2 = breathe and fully exhale in that zone. relevance: gives Landon the neutral-trunk position he must hold while the hips rotate (separation prerequisite)

drill_or_video_name: Problem Solving Your Front Leg Mechanics
description: Breaks down front-leg bracing as a commonly misunderstood concept — the lead leg straightens in RESPONSE to the back hip continuing to rotate around the front hip, not by actively pushing. Diagnostic for early front-side fly-open and a soft (non-blocking) front leg.
notes: relevance: diagnoses Landon's exact fault — lead leg lands open / no brace, so the block never decelerates the pelvis to whip the arm

description: The stride/lead leg must 'slam on the brakes' applying posterior force to stop momentum and catapult the body and ball toward the plate. Lead-leg posterior force correlates with velocity (back-leg drive does not). Explains the brace that converts hip drive into arm whip.
notes: THE article on the lead-leg block — the single most important feature for Landon (converts back-hip drive into a braced front side that whips the arm). relevance: conceptual/diagnostic basis for the lead-leg block

drill_or_video_name: Elevated Trail Leg Drill (Lead Leg Block Progression)
equipment: plyo ball, mound or flat ground
description: Trail leg elevated behind on a box so the athlete cannot push off; forces the front side to post and feel the natural sequencing of a lead-leg block instead of leaking back-hip energy. Front leg learns to brace and extend in response to pelvic rotation.
notes: Tread Lead Leg Block progression series — entry drill. relevance: directly trains the lead-leg block / front-side brace Landon lacks; removes back-hip push so he can't leak

drill_or_video_name: 6 Drills To Help Fix Your Front Leg
description: Six-drill front-leg sequence (posting, anti-collapse, brace-and-extend variations) to stop the lead leg from collapsing or landing open; builds a stable front side to rotate around.
notes: Tread front-leg drill compilation. relevance: multiple variations to fix lead-leg-block / front-side collapse — Landon's single most important feature

drill_or_video_name: Gain Velocity by Fixing Your Glove Arm (3 Glove Arm Drills)
description: Three glove-arm drills to stay closed: control the glove and pull the lead elbow down to the side as you rotate, holding the front side a beat longer rather than flying the glove open.
notes: relevance: glove-side discipline keeps the front side closed — directly attacks Landon's shoulders flying open early

description: Take walking steps with toes pointed at the target while MINIMIZING hip rotation; the throwing arm drives straight back as the stride foot contacts, then throw. Constrains the lower half to keep the front side closed and arm back.
notes: Throwing-program sequence member; 450g blue + 225g red plyo. Keeps toes/front side closed while loading the arm. relevance: trains staying closed and a delayed front side — the headline fix for early front-hip open

drill_or_video_name: Top 3 Drills for PERFECT Mechanics — Pec Sequence
description: Pre-set on the glove side with the ball resting on the throwing-side pec; from that loaded position fire hip-then-shoulder-then-arm in sequence so the trunk stays closed while the lower half initiates. Isolates the separation feel without a full delivery.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Top 3 Drills for PERFECT Mechanics)
notes: Cue: hip leads, trunk waits. relevance: trains hip-shoulder separation feel and keeps the trunk closed while the hips rotate — directly attacks shoulders-fly-open

description: Quick-pick constraint drill highlighted to clean up sequencing and timing into a closed front side; fast rep to groove rotate-late pattern.
notes: Tread drill highlight. relevance: grooves late rotation/closed sequencing — counters Landon's early front-hip swing

description: Pivot-pickoff constraint throw: counter-rotate and hang the throwing hand back, then drive the back hip and wrist over the elbow. Forces the pelvis to lead while the trunk stays back, building elite hip-shoulder separation and forward-rotation sequencing.
notes: Surfaced by Tread (Staying Closed) + Driveline (Hip Rotation, https://www.drivelinebaseball.com/2017/12/plyo-velocity-weighted-balls-replication/ ; 1kg green + 450g blue plyo) + Bauer (Arm Path plyo). High-confidence cross-source staple. relevance: builds the coil + separation Landon never opens AND back-hip drive before the arm

description: Side-lying with knees tucked, reach top arm open and rotate the T-spine while the floor limits ROM; drives scap retraction/depression and humeral external rotation. Entry-level T-spine rotation drill.
notes: T-spine extension/rotation should drive scap retraction, not lumbar rotation. relevance: builds the T-spine rotation capacity that lets Landon's trunk stay neutral/closed while the hips rotate (separation prerequisite)

description: Quadruped or bent-over, hand behind head, rotate the upper back open and closed isolating thoracic rotation while bracing the core so the lumbar spine stays quiet.
notes: Keep ribs down so motion comes from T-spine not low back. relevance: trains thoracic rotation independent of the pelvis — the exact capacity Landon needs to separate trunk from hips

drill_or_video_name: 7 Drills To Increase Hip To Shoulder Separation
description: Seven constraint drills to grow hip-shoulder separation: pelvis-underneath-neutral-trunk and counter-rotation variations; keep the front shoulder closed while the pelvis rotates.
notes: Tread separation series. relevance: trains trunk-neutral-while-hips-rotate — exactly the separation Landon can't create

drill_or_video_name: How To Feel Hip-Shoulder Separation (band + counter-rotation constraints)
description: Two stacked constraints for pitchers who can't feel pelvis/trunk independence: (1) band tension to open the back hip, (2) a counter-rotation component. Cue: relax, don't force the hips through.
notes: relevance: back-hip band tension + counter-rotation are the exact constraints for Landon's leaked back hip + lost separation

description: Walks through modifications of the pivot pickoff to scale the separation/coil demand and clean up sequencing into landing; high-speed slow-mo reference of the neutral vertical forearm and forward-rotation pattern to copy.
notes: Tread Pivot Pickoff series; same slow-mo URL also used by Driveline + Bauer as a form-model reference (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=espdyF-BEUU). relevance: scalable separation/coil work targeting Landon's no-coil, early-open pattern

description: A walker step into the leg kick using momentum directed at the target, chaining back-chaining drill work into a full-delivery feel while keeping the front side closed. LAST drill in the Driveline plyo routine and the Tread Walking Windup transition drill.
notes: Surfaced by Tread (Walking Windup series) + Driveline (final drill in plyo throwing-program sequence, https://www.drivelinebaseball.com/2017/12/plyo-velocity-weighted-balls-replication/ ). relevance: integrates staying-closed + momentum into a delivery-like pattern before transfer; bridges the drop->rotate transition Landon fails

description: Constraint drill (studied in Driveline's drill-kinematics work) that constrains the lower half/arm path to feel sequencing — counter-rotation and delayed trunk rotation while the arm works back.
notes: Constraint-drill family used in Driveline drill-constraint kinematics studies. relevance: trains delayed trunk rotation / staying closed while hips lead

description: Posterior pelvic tilt curling the pelvis toward the ribs without spinal hyperextension; teaches active control of the ribs-to-pelvis relationship under a little load.
notes: Bring pelvis and rib cage together; controls anterior pelvic tilt. relevance: anti-extension core strength so Landon doesn't dump into extension and fly open at landing

description: Loaded lead-hip IR so the front hip can pull back/rotate around a posted leg and the pelvis can clear without the front side flying open.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (2/7). relevance: front-hip IR lets the lead leg block instead of landing open — directly targets Landon's early fly-open

description: Same-side cable row to train connection between the front side and trunk — reinforces holding the front side/trunk closed against rotational pull.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (6/7). relevance: front-side connection so the trunk stays neutral/closed while the hips rotate (separation)

drill_or_video_name: Throw Harder by Mastering the Lead Leg Block [Full Routine]
description: Full lead-leg-block routine: progresses from constraint pattern work into loaded throws emphasizing high ground reaction force and front-knee extension at max external rotation. Front leg posts to decelerate the pelvis and redirect energy up the chain.
notes: Tread Lead Leg Block 'Full Routine' series. relevance: the headline fix for Landon — converts hip drive into a braced front side that whips, not pushes, the arm

drill_or_video_name: Throw Harder By Mastering Hip-Shoulder Separation [Full Routine]
description: Full separation routine: counter-rotation and band-tension constraints to feel the pelvis rotating underneath a neutral, closed trunk, progressing into loaded throws. Trunk stays closed while hips fire.
notes: Tread Hip-Shoulder Separation 'Full Routine' series. relevance: restores the separation window Landon loses when his shoulders fly open with the hips

drill_or_video_name: Creating Shoulder/Hip Separation | Tips with Trev Ep 21
description: Bauer explains what hip-shoulder separation is and why delaying the front side opening adds torque — keep the front side closed while the hips rotate so the shoulders arrive late. Conceptual breakdown plus on-mound demonstration.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Tips with Trev Ep 21)
notes: Headline concept video for Landon. Cue: delay the front side, fire the back hip. relevance: defines the exact separation window Landon loses when his front side flies open early

drill_or_video_name: Throw Harder By Using Hip Shoulder Separation
description: Explainer + drill on how separation is created and timed (positive separation at foot strike); diagnoses shoulders flying open early with the pelvis.
notes: relevance: identifies Landon's lost separation (shoulders open with hips) and the late-trunk fix

drill_or_video_name: A Different Model for Cueing Pitching Mechanics
description: Minimize internal cues, prefer external-focus cues, and use the plyo-ball wall to separate movement work from throwing. To get hip-shoulder separation the hips must rotate open while the trunk stays closed at footstrike; anti-rotation core stability enables it.
notes: Coaching framework for the separation cue. relevance: defines the staying-closed mechanism (hips open, trunk closed at footstrike) that Landon loses when shoulders fly open

description: Anti-rotation core stability lets athletes keep the trunk closed while the lower half and hips rotate open — the stability side of hip-shoulder separation.
notes: Stability prerequisite for separation. relevance: anti-rotation trunk stability is what stops Landon's shoulders flying open at landing

description: Overhead ROM depends on scapular upward rotation and T-spine extension; priorities are pain-free arm-overhead, sufficient GH internal/external rotation, and open hips to create hip-to-scap separation.
notes: Mobility prerequisites for separation. relevance: identifies the T-spine/hip mobility Landon needs to keep the trunk neutral while hips rotate

description: Cressey's off-season T-spine progression for throwers: side-lying windmill, bent-over rotation, foam-roller extension. T-spine delivers the scapula; a deficit lets the humeral head migrate forward and stress the pec.
notes: Inadequate T-spine mobility = forward humeral migration + pec strain. relevance: the T-spine library that underpins Landon's staying-closed and clean arm-path prerequisites

drill_or_video_name: How Rib Cage Positioning Impacts the Pitching Delivery (article)
description: An extended posture with poor rib-cage/pelvic control makes it hard to rotate efficiently at front-foot-strike while holding the line to home. Poor control = misses up and flat breaking balls.
notes: Thorax is the center of force transfer; uncontrolled rib cage prevents efficient rotation at foot strike. relevance: directly explains Landon's shoulders-fly-open / trunk-not-neutral fault as a rib-cage-control problem

drill_or_video_name: Pitching Performance: Trunk Position at Foot Strike (article)
description: Cressey on what good trunk position at front-foot-strike looks like and the strength/control that produces it — stacked rib cage over pelvis, controlled lateral and rotational lean.
notes: Trunk must arrive stacked and controlled at foot strike. relevance: the strength-coach picture of the braced, neutral trunk Landon currently loses when his shoulders fly open

drill_or_video_name: Pitching Mechanics: Is Lead Leg Blocking Enough? (article)
description: Cressey argues a good lead-leg block still fails to transfer force if limited upstream — chiefly inadequate thoracic flexion and scapular upward rotation. Connects the block to the chain above it.
notes: The block alone isn't enough without T-spine + scap upward rotation upstream. relevance: tells Landon WHY a braced front side won't whip the arm unless his T-spine/scap prerequisites are also fixed

drill_or_video_name: Why We Don't Teach Equal and Opposite / Firm Front Side
description: A glove-blocking 'firm front side' that stops deceleration short kills shoulder angular rotational velocity and adds shoulder/neck stress. The lead leg blocks but the glove side should not jam — nuance on front-side discipline.
notes: Nuance: lead-LEG block yes, glove-jam no. relevance: refines Landon's front-side fix — block with the leg, keep the glove/trunk discipline without over-blocking

drill_or_video_name: How To Throw Harder | Tips with Trev Ep 22
description: Bauer: the more you delay hips and shoulders opening while moving toward home, the more tension/torque you store; fire the back hip with the front side closed for more impulse at release. Ties separation directly to velocity.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Tips with Trev Ep 22)
notes: Cue: torque = delayed opening. relevance: explains why Landon's early-open front side bleeds velocity — staying closed is the fix

description: How Driveline reads a biomech report — staying stacked/closed (negative trunk angle/tilt at foot plant) can produce ~48 degrees hip-shoulder separation (elite); separation ties to trunk rotation velocity and pitch velocity.
notes: Diagnostic frame for separation metrics. relevance: gives the target (stacked/closed at foot plant -> big separation) that Landon's early-open pattern fails to hit

description: Weight-room lead-leg-block builder: loaded hip flexion biased to the hamstring to strengthen the front-hip's ability to absorb and post against landing forces.
notes: Tread 'lead leg block exercises in the weight room' list (1/7). relevance: builds front-hip capacity to brace — prerequisite for the lead-leg block Landon can't currently hold

description: Single-leg hip airplane to train controlled hip IR/ER and single-leg stability — the front-leg posting/balance demand of a lead-leg block.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (3/7). relevance: trains the front leg to post and rotate around without collapsing — the brace Landon lacks

description: Split-stance med-ball taps in a stride-like position to train front-leg bracing and rhythmic rotation off a stable front side.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (5/7). relevance: stride-specific bracing rep — front leg posts while trunk rotates, mirroring the block Landon needs

drill_or_video_name: Fixing Your Lead Leg Block: Hip Rotation w/ Strap
description: Strap anchored to drive hip rotation into a braced front leg; teaches the back hip to clear/rotate AROUND a posted front leg rather than drifting open. The front leg blocks and extends as the pelvis rotates through.
notes: Tread Lead Leg Block series — strap-constraint variation. relevance: fixes the leaking back hip + open front-leg landing; trains rotate-into-block, Landon's #1/#2 seam

drill_or_video_name: Static 45 Skier (Top 3 Drills for PERFECT Mechanics)
description: Split stance with back foot at 45 degrees and heel off the ground; load the glove side then rotate/fire the back hip and throw. Trains the back hip to drive and clear rather than leak.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Top 3 Drills for PERFECT Mechanics)
notes: Cue: fire and clear the back hip. relevance: fixes back-hip leak / drop-no-rotate transition by isolating trail-hip drive

drill_or_video_name: Posture Squat (Top 3 Drills for PERFECT Mechanics)
description: Feet ~shoulder width, squat as low as possible to load both hips, then kick the lead leg and rotate — combines the Pec Sequence and Static 45 Skier into one seamless drop-to-rotate transition.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Top 3 Drills for PERFECT Mechanics)
notes: Bauer's most important of the three — chains the other two. relevance: directly trains the good-drop-then-rotate sequence Landon is missing

description: Start side-on; counter-rotate and hang the throwing hand back, then drive the back hip and wrist over the elbow into the wall. Trains forward rotation sequencing, medial-forearm strength, and pronation timing. Performed early in the plyo routine after reverse throws.
notes: Throwing-program sequence member; 1kg green + 450g blue plyo. Forces back-hip drive before arm — directly targets the drop-to-rotate failure. relevance: trains back-hip rotation/clearing so the trail hip stops leaking energy. NOTE: same drill family as the Staying-Closed Pivot Pickoff row — this row is the heavier-ball / back-hip-drive variant

description: Teaches the back-hip hinge/load so the pelvis can rotate from a hinged, loaded position rather than lunging or pushing off the back leg.
notes: relevance: builds the loaded back-hip position Landon gets (good drop) and teaches converting it to rotation, not a leak

description: Face the target, take one step back with the drive leg, then step forward to throw. Simplifies the load so the athlete can feel back-leg engagement before adding momentum.
notes: Constraint-drill family; entry-level back-leg loader. relevance: isolates trail-leg drive feeding the hip rotation Landon stalls on

description: Step back with the back leg, gather into a coil keeping the back hip externally rotated and the outside of the foot anchored, then throw. Anchors the back leg and improves glute/hamstring activation into the drive.
notes: Throwing-program sequence member; precursor to Walking Windups. relevance: anchors back hip in external rotation so it can rotate/clear instead of leaking

drill_or_video_name: TRX Drift Drill (Struggling to Feel Your Lower Half)
description: TRX-supported drift drill to feel loading into the back hip and sequencing the lower half without falling forward; isolates the back-leg load.
notes: relevance: TRX constraint helps Landon feel the back-hip load before rotation, reducing the early leak

description: Set feet wider than stride with front foot slightly closed; rock back and forth 1-3 times to load the back leg and create rhythm, then throw to a target. Trains weight shift, back-leg loading, center-of-mass control and front-leg strength. Prescribed a few weeks in after other mechanics clean up.
notes: Surfaced by Tread + Driveline (throwing-program sequence member, finishes in a delivery-like position, https://www.drivelinebaseball.com/2017/12/plyo-velocity-weighted-balls-replication/ ). relevance: cleans up drift/weight-shift so the back hip loads to rotate into a braced front leg instead of leaking forward

drill_or_video_name: Lying Knee-to-Knee Stretch (hip IR / adductor mobility family)
description: Supine, soles together; actively internally rotate the femurs so the stretch occurs at the hips not the knees. Direct hip IR mobilization. Coach the IR action to avoid knee valgus stress.
notes: Anchor of Cressey's hip-IR / adductor-mobility family — interchangeable variants folded here: Supine Dynamic Hip IR Stretch (https://ericcressey.com/flexibility-deficits-in-pitchers/), Split-Stance Kneeling Adductor Mob (https://ericcressey.com/5-strategies-quickly-increasing-mobility/), Left-Stance Toe Touch (https://ericcressey.com/mobility-exercise-of-the-week-left-stance-toe-touch/), Table Adductor Dips (https://ericcressey.com/mobility-exercise-of-the-week-table-adductor-dips/). Actively IR the femurs — stretch is at the hip, never let the knees collapse. relevance: opens lead-leg hip internal rotation so the front hip can post instead of swinging open early

description: Half-kneeling drill that tactfully works hip internal rotation by rotating over a fixed femur without cranking the joint. One of Cressey's favorite hip-IR drills.
notes: Work hip IR without cranking the joint. relevance: low-stress hip-IR builder for the lead-leg-block / front-hip-post prerequisite

description: Half-kneeling hip-flexor/anterior-hip stretch against the wall to restore hip extension while keeping ribs down and core braced (no lumbar arch).
notes: Get hip extension without arching the low back. relevance: hip-extension deficit forces a lumbar-arch compensation that throws Landon's trunk into extension and open

description: Rotational kettlebell swing to train ballistic transverse-plane hip drive and clearing — back-hip rotation power feeding into a braced front side.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (4/7). relevance: trains back-hip rotation/clearing power so the hip drives the block rather than leaking

description: Multi-plane strap rotations driving hip-led rotation through the front-leg block in all three planes; patterns rotate-into-brace.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (7/7). relevance: rehearses hip rotation INTO a posted front leg — the rotate-then-block sequence Landon misses

drill_or_video_name: Lateral Lunge with Band Overhead Reach
description: Combo drill: lateral lunge loading the adductors/back hip while a banded overhead reach challenges rotary + anterior core — mimics riding the back hip down the mound under control.
notes: Ride the back hip down while controlling the trunk. relevance: pairs back-hip loading with anti-extension core — exactly the back-hip-drive-without-leaking pattern Landon needs

description: Glute bridge to terminal hip extension while reaching the arms back to add thoracic rotation/mobility — couples hip extension with T-spine motion as happens in delivery.
notes: Hips extend AND T-spine keeps rotating. relevance: links posterior-chain hip extension with T-spine rotation — the back-hip-drive-into-separation coupling

description: Single-leg hinge with a rotational reach across the planted leg; trains glute med/posterior-chain control of the pelvis on one leg under a frontal/rotational demand.
notes: Control the pelvis on one leg — don't let the knee cave. relevance: builds the single-leg glute-med strength that braces the lead leg block and stabilizes back-hip drive

description: Split-stance glute-med activation lifting the leg at end range to strengthen the hip abductors/external rotators that keep the knee from caving in single-leg.
notes: Glute med weakness = knee collapses medially in single-leg. relevance: strengthens the abductors that keep the lead leg from collapsing/landing open in the block

drill_or_video_name: Fix Your Lower Half Mechanics | Tips with Trev Ep 3
description: Bauer teaches lower-half mechanics: back-hip load, hip drive toward the plate, lead-leg landing and the front-side block. Walks through how the lower half should sequence into the throw.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Tips with Trev Ep 3)
notes: Cue: drive the back hip, post the front leg. relevance: targets back-hip leak and the lead-leg block Landon never establishes

description: Bauer film note: back knee rolls downward to keep the hips in play until footstrike, allowing the hips to rotate and lead the torso — count the frames the hips are stopped while the torso keeps turning. Also shows more efficient blocking.
notes: His own diagnostic of the exact fault. relevance: directly addresses hips-lead-torso sequencing AND lead-leg block (efficient blocking) — Landon's two biggest gaps

drill_or_video_name: \"Using Your Legs\" — Ground Reaction Forces and Energy Flow
description: Explains how a good lead leg block produces high ground reaction forces and redirects back-leg momentum up the chain to torso and arm; frames why a leaking back hip + soft front leg bleeds velocity. Conceptual primer for the block.
notes: relevance: explains why Landon's leaked back hip + open front leg = no energy transfer to the arm (push, not whip)

drill_or_video_name: Drive Leg Mechanics to Increase Velocity & Reduce Arm Stress
description: Breaks down rear-glute/hamstring drive (pull the ground back like a sprinter) vs quad push; back-hip drive that reduces arm stress.
notes: relevance: trains back-hip DRIVE (glute/ham) to stop the leak; lower arm stress matters for Landon's flexor-pronator history

drill_or_video_name: Problem Solving Your Lower Half Pitching Mechanics
description: Cornerstone lower-half article: rear-glute drive, step-back drills, rotate vs lunge off the back leg, and front-leg brace in response to hip rotation.
notes: relevance: covers Landon's whole lower-half chain — back-hip drive, rotate-not-lunge, and the lead-leg brace

drill_or_video_name: The \"Drift\" — How to Shift Your Weight Like a Pro
description: Article on shifting weight into the back hip correctly so the lower half can sequence; addresses drifting too early and losing the load.
notes: relevance: fixes early drift/leak that opens Landon's front side before the separation window

drill_or_video_name: Re-thinking How the Back Hip Works Into Landing
description: Reframes the unload: the pelvis (middle of the body) drives rotation into landing, not the rear femur slamming into IR. Better mental model for clearing the back hip.
notes: relevance: corrects how Landon should think about the back-hip unload so it rotates/clears instead of leaking

drill_or_video_name: How Strength and Mobility Impact the Pitching Stride (article)
description: Cressey on the posterior chain as a counterbalance: engage glutes/hamstrings instead of swinging the front leg into landing — start like a one-leg squat to feel glute/ham, then lateral lunge to keep adductors controlling the pelvis.
notes: Lead leg as counter-balance, not the primary power source. relevance: the strength-coach blueprint for the lead-leg block — stop swinging the front leg open, use the posterior chain to control the stride

drill_or_video_name: Analyzing Front and Back Leg Ground Forces
description: Research article: stride-leg ground reaction force during arm-cocking/acceleration strongly correlates with ball velocity (r-squared 0.45-0.61); back-leg 'drive' does NOT significantly correlate. Peak stride-leg force in arm-cocking is the best velocity predictor.
notes: Evidence base for prioritizing the lead-leg block over back-leg push. relevance: quantifies why fixing Landon's front-side brace (not just back-leg drive) is what unlocks velocity

drill_or_video_name: Range of Motion, Hip-Shoulder Separation & Kinematics (study)
description: Open-access study (IJSPT, PMC7727427): hip-shoulder separation at foot contact, peak pelvis velocity, and the timing of peak pelvis velocity each individually predict peak trunk velocity; peak trunk velocity predicts ball velocity. Pelvis peaks ~12% of stride-to-release, trunk ~36%.
notes: Core kinematic-sequencing evidence (pelvis->trunk->arm timing). relevance: explains EXACTLY Landon's problem — separation at foot contact + pelvis-leading-trunk timing drives velocity; his early-open pattern collapses this sequence

description: Forearms on wall shoulder-width, elbows 90deg, slide up and out without shrugging; band cues serratus/lower-trap to drive scapular upward rotation so the upper trap doesn't dominate.
notes: Slide up and out, no shrug — serratus + lower trap do the rotating. relevance: scap upward-rotation deficit keeps the hand from getting out front; this cleans the arm path off a braced front side

description: Progression of wall-slide variants (forearm, J-band, med-ball) building dynamic scapular upward rotation and serratus control for overhead positions.
notes: Serratus is the rotation component of scapular upward rotation. relevance: pitchers need more scap upward rotation than any other athlete — this is the arm-path prerequisite for whip not push

description: Stand back to wall, ribs down, raise arms overhead keeping low back flat and ribs down; exposes whether shoulder flexion comes from the scap/T-spine or a lumbar-arch compensation.
notes: Reach overhead without arching the back or flaring the ribs. relevance: screens/trains overhead capacity so the arm can lay back off a stacked trunk instead of an extended one

description: Driveline breakdown of the towel drill, position holds, and the up-down-out arm-action pattern — feeling the arm path and layback positions without throwing a ball.
notes: Constraint/feel work for arm action. relevance: grooves a whip/spiral arm path and layback to replace the push arm action

drill_or_video_name: How to Sync the Arm into the Plane of Shoulder Rotation
description: Teaches keeping the arm IN the plane of shoulder rotation instead of finishing straight down when the torso flexes to 'throw strikes'; lets the trunk rotate the arm through in plane (spiral, not push).
notes: relevance: converts Landon's push/out-of-plane finish into an in-plane spiral driven by trunk rotation

description: Driveline video walking through the plyocare drill set (reverse throws, pivot pickoffs, roll-ins, rockers) with technique cues for each.
notes: Video index of the plyocare drill family. relevance: visual reference for the constraint drills that train staying closed + arm path

description: Cressey video on dynamic scapular control for the overhead athlete — coordinating cuff and scap stabilizers through range rather than static holds.
notes: Train cuff and scap stabilizers together, dynamically. relevance: dynamic scap control is what lets the arm spiral cleanly instead of the shoulder flying open and pushing

description: Kneel on throwing-arm-side knee, hold plyo ball low, counter-rotate the torso and drive the arm back through the slot to throw against a wall behind you. Trains the posterior shoulder and scap to accept high-velocity deceleration force plus T-spine mobility. Always the FIRST drill in the Driveline plyo routine.
notes: Surfaced by Driveline (Arm Path, first drill in plyo sequence, 450g/225g balls) + Bauer (Velocity/CNS deceleration staple, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDd2yMSSnY ). High-confidence arm-care staple. relevance: builds posterior-shoulder/scap durability + layback so the arm whips instead of pushing; underload-friendly, keep <=6oz/450g soft plyo

drill_or_video_name: Fix Your Upper Half Mechanics | Tips with Trev Ep 4
description: Bauer covers upper-half mechanics: glove-side discipline, trunk posture, and arm action — keeping the upper half from opening prematurely so the arm whips rather than pushes.
source: Trevor Bauer YouTube (Tips with Trev Ep 4)
notes: Cue: don't mess up the upper half once the lower half is right. relevance: glove-side/trunk discipline that keeps shoulders from flying open

description: Prone on incline, raise arm to a Y to strengthen lower trap and correct an anteriorly-tilted scapula; troubleshooting cues keep the upper trap quiet.
notes: Lower trap drives it; keep the upper trap from taking over. relevance: corrects anterior scap tilt so the scapula upwardly rotates and posts the arm — clean arm path prerequisite

description: Prone posterior-cuff drill: externally rotate the humeral head without anterior migration; gravity pulls the scap into anterior tilt so the lower trap must fire to counteract.
notes: ER the humeral head without it sliding forward; lower trap holds the scap. relevance: posterior-cuff + scap control that protects the shoulder and supports layback in the arm path

drill_or_video_name: Half-Kneeling Cable External Rotation - Scapular Plane
description: Half-kneeling cable ER in the scapular plane; the half-kneel locks the lower body so the cuff/scap work in a pitching-relevant plane with anti-rotation core demand.
notes: Work the cuff in the scapular plane, core braced. relevance: scap-plane cuff strength + anti-rotation core = the stable shoulder/trunk that lets the arm whip not push

drill_or_video_name: One-Arm Band Rotational Row from Low Setting
description: Low-to-high rotational row emphasizing getting the hand up-and-out in front; Cressey programs it as the last drill before a pitcher picks up the ball.
notes: Get up and out in front. relevance: grooves the up-and-out-front scap/arm action — the pre-throw primer for getting the hand out front instead of pushing

description: From a pickoff-style stance, throw the plyo ball with a neutral, somewhat-vertical forearm — ball faces the same way as the chest. Cleans up forward rotation in the delivery and builds medial forearm strength. Keep weights light given elbow history.

notes: Bauer staple; arm-path/forearm framing of the pivot pickoff (cross-referenced with the Tread/Driveline Pivot Pickoff rows above). Watch forearm position with heavier balls. relevance: grooves forward rotation + arm path while strengthening flexor-pronator — protective for Landon's medial elbow; cap at 6oz

drill_or_video_name: How to Fix a Pushing Arm Action — 7 Causes and Solutions
description: Diagnoses 7 causes of a pushing arm action (too-heavy balls, fake layback via elbow flexion, lost hip-shoulder separation, tight lats/triceps, injury compensation) and the fixes — keep shoulders closed at landing so the arm stays in line and whips.
notes: relevance: directly addresses Landon's PUSH arm action; note the injury-compensation cause given his flexor-pronator history — keep ball weights <=6oz

drill_or_video_name: The 3 Types of \"Muscling Up\" — How It Hurts Your Velocity
description: Explains three ways pitchers muscle up the arm (tense/push) instead of letting the trunk reflexively whip the arm into release; cue to relax the arm and let pecs/lats fire reflexively.
notes: relevance: names the tense/push habit Landon defaults to without a braced front side; primes the whip feel

drill_or_video_name: Bauer + Yu Darvish Arm Action Mechanics
description: Side-by-side arm-action breakdown — Bauer's elite external-rotation-to-internal-rotation transition and how the trunk pulls the arm into layback rather than the arm pushing. Notes the scap-load disconnect to watch for.

description: Breakdown of how spin axis determines pitch movement, using Bauer's arsenal — the conceptual basis for designing release and finger drive.

description: Bauer demonstrates his grips and the finger placement / pressure that shapes spin axis on each pitch.

description: Three-round constraint warmup: Round 1 heavy balls (450g-2kg) at 25-50% intent, Round 2 medium (150g-450g) at 50-75%, Round 3 lightest (100g-225g) at 50-75%. Sub-max constraint work every throwing day; never max intent.
notes: Daily sub-max constraint routine; lightest 100g ball ~3.5oz underload CNS work is Landon-appropriate. 2kg/1kg heavy plyos are HEAVY but used at low intent for pattern, not max-intent overload. Lower-intensity variants folded here from same source: Recovery-Day Plyo (50-60% intent, light 100-225g balls only) and In-Season Pre-Game Plyo (controlled-intent prime, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V5ACgxa-tXk). relevance: low-stress daily pattern work for staying closed + arm path without medial-elbow load

description: Velocity-based training in the weight room — using bar-speed feedback to autoregulate power/strength work that underpins throwing velocity.

drill_or_video_name: 13 Tips for Mighty Elbows and Wrists (article)
description: Cressey's elbow/wrist durability tips: wrist flexion/extension, pronation/supination, radial/ulnar deviation, grip, and soft-tissue work on the flexor mass; balance the forearm musculature.
notes: Train all forearm actions + soft tissue the flexors. relevance: directly addresses Landon's flexor-pronator / medial-elbow durability — the conservative-load non-negotiable

description: Elastic-band resisted forearm pronation; research-validated to selectively activate the flexor-pronator mass that dynamically stabilizes the elbow against valgus.

description: Resisted ulnar deviation targeting flexor carpi ulnaris, the biggest dynamic protector of the UCL during throwing.

description: Self soft-tissue work on the palmar fascia and anteromedial forearm flexors to manage tissue quality; pair with Graston/ART-style work for symptomatic elbows.

drill_or_video_name: Comparing Overload vs. Underload Weighted Ball Stress
description: Research article: insufficient evidence that under- or over-load implements are more stressful than a 5oz ball at max effort. Underload moves the arm faster with less force; overload slower with more force. Driveline rarely exceeds 9oz overload, never below 3oz underload.
notes: year-2 / not-now for the overload (7-9oz) side. Underload 3-4oz is Landon-appropriate. relevance: justifies using 3-4oz underload for arm speed while deferring 7oz+ overload due to flexor-pronator history

description: Sit tall, dowel across shoulders, rotate trunk each direction; minimum standard is 45 degrees per side. Pitchers with less than 45deg to the throwing side compensate with lumbar rotation or scap protraction.
notes: Assess T-spine rotation before prescribing. relevance: a sub-45deg deficit forces the trunk to fly open instead of separating, so this is the gate test for Landon's staying-closed / separation prerequisite

description: Sit at edge of table, hips/knees at 90, internally rotate the femur without letting the hip hike. Compares lead-leg HIR to back-leg HIR; a lead-leg deficit is common in pitchers with elbow/shoulder pain.
notes: Identifies hip IR deficit (HIRD). relevance: lead-leg HIR deficit makes the front hip open early and the lead leg land open — the root of Landon's lost separation window

description: Cressey's catalog of the key pitcher deficits: lead-leg hip internal rotation deficit, hip extension deficit, GIRD, and T-spine restriction. Frames which limitations force mechanical compensations.
notes: When front hip opens too soon from muscular restriction, the arm lags out of the scapular plane. relevance: maps Landon's whole fault chain (early front side, push arm) to specific physical deficits to screen

description: Defines separation as the pelvis and trunk separating: created by the pelvis rotating under a neutral trunk, the trunk counter-rotating, or both. Conceptual diagnostic.
notes: relevance: gives Landon/coach the model for what separation should feel like before drilling it

description: Cressey on the common flexor tendon anatomy (FCR, FCU, palmaris, FDS, pronator teres) and how the flexor-pronator mass dynamically stabilizes the elbow against valgus load.
notes: The flexor-pronator mass is the dynamic valgus stabilizer of the elbow. relevance: explains WHY pronator/flexor strengthening protects Landon's medial elbow — the rationale for the durability block
